notes: Direct deps (5): root -> app (workspace/local), root -> lib (workspace/local), app -> zod (catalog: expanded to 3.25.68), app -> react (catalog:react18 expanded to 18.3.1), app -> react-dom (catalog:react18 expanded to 18.3.1). lib -> zod is deduplicated with app -> zod (same resolved node).
Transitive deps (3): scheduler@0.23.2 (via react-dom), loose-envify@1.4.0 (via react and scheduler, deduplicated), js-tokens@4.0.0 (via loose-envify).
zod@3.25.68 is referenced by both app and lib via 'catalog:' — Mend should deduplicate to one node in the graph.

If Mend counts workspace members (app, lib) as sub-project roots rather than as dep nodes, the direct count from the root perspective would be 2 (app, lib) and each sub-project would carry its own direct count. Either representation is acceptable; catalog: expansion correctness is the key assertion.
Mend may report per-workspace-member trees. In that case: app direct=3 (zod, react, react-dom), lib direct=1 (zod), total direct across members=4, total transitive=3.

catalog_token_left_literal.description: If Mend does not recognize 'catalog:' as a version-specifier type, the dep will appear with version string 'catalog:' or 'catalog:react18' verbatim rather than the expanded concrete version.
catalog_token_left_literal.symptom: zod appears at version 'catalog:' instead of '3.25.68'; react appears at 'catalog:react18' instead of '18.3.1'.

catalog_deps_dropped.description: Older Mend parsers may treat 'catalog:' and 'catalog:react18' as unknown specifier tokens and drop the dep entirely from the tree.
catalog_deps_dropped.symptom: zod, react, and/or react-dom missing from app sub-tree; zod missing from lib sub-tree.

named_catalog_not_found.description: Parser may expand the default 'catalog:' correctly but fail to resolve 'catalog:<name>' named references.
named_catalog_not_found.symptom: zod present at correct version; react and react-dom absent or at literal version string.

catalog_field_ignored.description: If the parser ignores the root 'catalog' and 'catalogs' fields in package.json entirely, it has no expansion table and all catalog: tokens fail. This is the most likely gap for parsers built before Bun 1.2.
catalog_field_ignored.symptom: All catalog-sourced deps absent or unversioned.

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver (js-bun is Bucket C in whitesource-config.md). No .whitesource file is emitted — manual tool version pinning via scanSettings.versioning is impossible for this ecosystem. Detection is lockfile-driven (bun.lock JSONC parsing).

CATALOG EXPANSION REQUIREMENT (D4): The 'catalog:' version token in packages/app/package.json and packages/lib/package.json MUST be expanded to the concrete version declared in the root package.json 'catalog' block (zod ^3.25.0 -> 3.25.68). If Mend's bun.lock parser does not recognize the 'catalog:' specifier, zod will appear with literal version 'catalog:' — a clear parse-error signal.

NAMED CATALOG EXPANSION REQUIREMENT (D5): The 'catalog:react18' token in packages/app/package.json MUST be expanded via the root package.json 'catalogs.react18' block (react ^18.0.0 -> 18.3.1, react-dom ^18.0.0 -> 18.3.1). Named catalogs are a separate code path from the default 'catalog:'. A parser can pass D4 and still fail D5 if named-catalog lookup is unimplemented.

OPEN QUESTION Q3 (docs/BUN_COVERAGE_PLAN.md §9): 'Does Mend understand Bun 1.2 catalog / catalogs?' This probe is the designated instrument. If the probe's actual scan output shows catalog-token literals or missing deps, file a Mend bug linked from this probe README.

BUN 1.2+ MINIMUM: 'catalog' and 'catalogs' fields are Bun 1.2+ features. Any environment running an older Bun toolchain conceptually (lockfile-only static parser) must still recognize these manifest fields from the written bun.lock content — there is no UA pre-step to install the right Bun version.

DEDUPLICATION: zod is declared in both packages/app and packages/lib with 'catalog:', resolving to the same version (3.25.68). Mend must deduplicate to one graph node. If Mend reports two separate zod entries at different versions, that indicates a catalog-resolution + deduplication failure.

TRANSITIVE CORRECTNESS: react@18.3.1 and react-dom@18.3.1 pull in scheduler@0.23.2, loose-envify@1.4.0, and js-tokens@4.0.0 as transitives. If catalog expansion is broken (deps dropped or unversioned), these transitives will also be absent. An empty transitive count combined with absent catalog deps confirms catalog-expansion failure.

This probe is the designated instrument for answering docs/BUN_COVERAGE_PLAN.md §9 open question (3). Record the observed Mend output in the probe README and BUN_COVERAGE_PLAN.md §11.4 row #21 before closing.
